name: Suspicious PowerShell command features
description: Identifies process creation for PowerShell with encoded-command or remote-content indicators. Administrative scripts can use the same features and require contextual review.

attack.name: Command and Scripting Interpreter: PowerShell
attack.mapping_condition: Apply when PowerShell is used for adversary execution, not merely because powershell.exe ran.

query.text:
index=<configured-index> sourcetype=XmlWinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational EventCode=1 Image="*\\powershell.exe"
| search CommandLine="*-enc*" OR CommandLine="*EncodedCommand*" OR CommandLine="*FromBase64String*" OR CommandLine="*DownloadString*" OR CommandLine="*Invoke-WebRequest*"
| table _time host User ParentImage Image CommandLine ProcessId ParentProcessId
| sort -_time

false_positives: Approved software deployment or endpoint-management scripts
Administrator troubleshooting that retrieves a known internal resource
triage: Preserve the complete command line and parent-child process chain.
Decode content in an isolated analysis environment without executing it.
Correlate file, DNS, network, scheduled-task, and account activity on the host.
